summary.intent: Run QA on the CHA-17 branch implementing crm.matters.* RPC filter/pagination methods, verify correctness, and ensure adequate test coverage before merging.
summary.outcome: QA passed with one coverage gap identified and fixed: 6 new service-level tests were added for the `list_matters` filter dispatch in `LiveCrmService`. All 496 tests pass, clippy is clean, and a fix commit was pushed.

repo: The project uses a pinned nightly Rust toolchain with `cargo fmt` (nightly) + `clippy -D warnings` as quality gates.
CRM list methods follow a pattern of accepting `params: Value` and dispatching to a `_filtered` store method; `crm.matters.list` was brought in line with this convention.
SQLite store uses dynamic SQL with manually constructed WHERE clauses and positional params (not a query-builder), following the same pattern as `crm.contacts` list.
The memory store uses `u64::MAX` as the sentinel for 'no limit' in `list_matters_filtered`.
A pre-existing compile issue exists in the `web` crate due to a missing `style.css` asset — unrelated to this branch.
New DB migrations add indexes on `phase` and `practice_area` columns to support efficient filtered queries.
The `service-traits` crate holds the `CrmService` trait; `LiveCrmService` lives in `crates/gateway/src/crm_service.rs`.

code.finding: The `has_filters` dispatch branch for `list_matters` had zero service-level test coverage before this QA run. Store-level filter tests existed but did not exercise parameter parsing or enum validation through the service layer.
Dynamic SQL construction for filtered list queries; 9 new tests were added at the store level covering filter combinations.
`u64::MAX` used as sentinel value for unbounded limit in in-memory store's `list_matters_filtered` implementation.

workflow: QA skill stashes untracked `.gitignore` changes added by the orchestration tooling (gstack) to satisfy a clean working-tree requirement before diffing.
QA scope is limited to changed crates (`cargo test -p crm -p gateway`) rather than the full workspace, working around the pre-existing `web` crate compile failure.
The Linear workpad pattern (single comment, append-only, milestone updates) is used as a persistent state store across autonomous agent runs.
Coverage gaps discovered during code review are fixed in-session and committed before the QA run is marked complete.

friction: Pre-existing compile failure in the `web` crate (missing `style.css`) prevents running `cargo build` or `cargo test` workspace-wide; QA had to scope to individual changed crates.
The `.gitignore` file was dirtied by the orchestration tooling (gstack) on session start, requiring a stash before the clean-tree check could pass.
open_items: Status transition validation (e.g., archived → open) is explicitly out of scope and deferred.
FTS5 full-text search for matters — deferred in favor of LIKE.
UUID validation on IDs is a pre-existing gap across all CRM methods — not addressed.
`crm.contacts.list` filtering has the same gap as matters did — tracked as a separate issue.
Fix the `web` crate compile error caused by the missing `style.css` asset.
